# 🗃️ SQL Helpers


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

## 🎯 Overview

This module provides a **batteries-included SQL toolkit** for building
database-heavy applications:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 37%" />
<col style="width: 27%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Category</th>
<th>Functions</th>
<th>Purpose</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>🔍 Query Registry</td>
<td><code>run_id</code>, <code>validate_params</code></td>
<td>Execute queries by ID from centralized registries</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>➕ Insert-Only</td>
<td><code>insert_only</code>, <code>bulk_insert_only</code></td>
<td>Insert new records, skip conflicts</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>🔄 Upsert</td>
<td><code>upsert</code>, <code>bulk_upsert</code></td>
<td>Insert or update existing records</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>📝 CRUD</td>
<td><code>get_by_id</code>, <code>update_record</code>,
<code>delete_record</code>, <code>bulk_delete</code></td>
<td>Standard database operations</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>🔧 Utilities</td>
<td><code>with_transaction</code>, <code>paginate_sql</code>,
<code>batch_execute</code></td>
<td>Transaction management &amp; helpers</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>💰 Money</td>
<td><code>to_cents</code>, <code>from_cents</code></td>
<td>Currency conversion for <code>int</code>-only storage</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 🏗️ Architecture

    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │                     SQL Utilities Layer                         │
    ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │  🔍 Query Registry        │  Execute queries by ID              │
    │  ➕ Insert Operations     │  insert_only, bulk_insert_only      │
    │  🔄 Upsert Operations     │  upsert, bulk_upsert                │
    │  📝 CRUD Operations       │  get, update, delete (single/bulk)  │
    │  🔧 Utilities             │  transactions, pagination, batching │
    │  💰 Money Helpers         │  cents ↔ dollars conversion         │
    ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │  DB_TYPE Detection: PostgreSQL / SQLite (auto-switch syntax)    │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  ▼
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │              fastsql Database Connection                        │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 📚 Quick Reference

### Conflict Handling

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 32%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 29%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Operation</th>
<th>On Conflict</th>
<th>Use Case</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>insert_only</code></td>
<td><strong>Skip</strong> (DO NOTHING)</td>
<td>Append-only logs, idempotent imports</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>upsert</code></td>
<td><strong>Update</strong> existing</td>
<td>Sync external data, refresh caches</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### Performance Tips

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Scenario</th>
<th>Recommended</th>
<th>Why</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Single record</td>
<td><code>insert_only</code>, <code>upsert</code></td>
<td>Simple, immediate</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>10+ records</td>
<td><code>bulk_*</code> variants</td>
<td>10-100x faster</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>100+ records</td>
<td><code>batch_execute</code></td>
<td>Commits per batch, memory-safe</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Multi-table changes</td>
<td><code>with_transaction</code></td>
<td>All-or-nothing commits</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 🔍 Database Type Detection

Automatically detects PostgreSQL vs SQLite from `DB_TYPE` environment
variable to generate appropriate SQL syntax.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### get_db_type

``` python

def get_db_type(
    
):

```

*Get database type from environment variable*

## 🎯 Query Registry

Execute queries by ID from a centralized registry with automatic
parameter validation.

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Function</th>
<th>Purpose</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>run_id</code></td>
<td>Execute a registered query by its ID</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>validate_params</code></td>
<td>Ensure all <code>:param</code> placeholders have values</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

``` python

def run_id(
    db:Database, registry:Dict, query_id:str, params:Optional=None
)->Any:

```

*Execute a query by ID from a query registry.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### validate_params

``` python

def validate_params(
    sql:str, params:Dict
)->None:

```

*Validate that all required parameters are provided*

## ➕ Insert-Only

Insert new records **only if they don’t exist** — conflicts are silently
skipped.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 29%" />
<col style="width: 26%" />
<col style="width: 44%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Function</th>
<th>Records</th>
<th>SQL Generated</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>insert_only</code></td>
<td>Single</td>
<td><code>ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING</code> (PG) /
<code>INSERT OR IGNORE</code> (SQLite)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>bulk_insert_only</code></td>
<td>Multiple</td>
<td>Same, with batch execution</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> 💡 **Use case**: Idempotent imports, append-only audit logs, webhook
> deduplication

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### bulk_insert_only

``` python

def bulk_insert_only(
    db:Database, table_name:str, records:List, conflict_cols:List, auto_commit:bool=True
)->None:

```

*Insert multiple records, skipping conflicts (optimized batch
operation).*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### insert_only

``` python

def insert_only(
    db:Database, table_name:str, record:Dict, conflict_cols:List, auto_commit:bool=True
)->None:

```

*Insert a single record only if it doesn’t exist (ignores conflicts).*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 🔄 Upsert

Insert new records **or update existing ones** on conflict.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 29%" />
<col style="width: 26%" />
<col style="width: 44%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Function</th>
<th>Records</th>
<th>SQL Generated</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>upsert</code></td>
<td>Single</td>
<td><code>ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE</code> (PG) /
<code>INSERT OR REPLACE</code> (SQLite)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>bulk_upsert</code></td>
<td>Multiple</td>
<td>Same, with batch execution</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> 💡 **Use case**: Syncing external API data, refreshing caches, user
> settings

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### bulk_upsert

``` python

def bulk_upsert(
    db:Database, table_name:str, records:List, conflict_cols:List, update_cols:Optional=None, auto_commit:bool=True
)->None:

```

*Insert or update multiple records (optimized batch operation).*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### upsert

``` python

def upsert(
    db:Database, table_name:str, record:Dict, conflict_cols:List, update_cols:Optional=None, auto_commit:bool=True
)->None:

```

*Insert a record or update if it exists (upsert).*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<td>Remove multiple records by ID list</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</tr>
</thead>

</table>

``` python
    db:Database, operation_func, items:List, batch_size:int=100
)->None:

```

*Execute an operation on items in batches with commits after each

``` python
)->str:
```

## 💰 Money Helpers

Convert between dollar amounts and integer cents for database storage.

> ⚠️ **Why cents?** fastsql only supports `int` and `str` types —
> storing money as cents avoids floating-point precision issues.

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Function</th>
<th>Direction</th>
<th>Example</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>to_cents</code></td>
<td><code>"$150.00"</code> → <code>15000</code></td>
<td>Store in DB</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>from_cents</code></td>
<td><code>15000</code> → <code>"$150.00"</code></td>
<td>Display to user</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### to_cents

``` python

def to_cents(
    dollars:str | float | None
)->int | None:

```

*Convert dollar amount to integer cents for database storage.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### from_cents

``` python

def from_cents(
    cents:int | None
)->str:

```

*Convert integer cents to formatted dollar string for display.*
